scope: Phase N1B uniformity audit of the native decode block tile (Hd=128,Hq=32,Hkv=8,MAXC=4608,L=96,S=48)
method: classify every int ADD/MUL/SHL/AND in the post-full_rewrite (pre-isel) sink; uniform iff all sources are CONST/RANGE/DEFINE_VAR/gidx-SPECIAL or (recursively) uniform; lane-varying if derived from lidx or a LOAD
proposed_lowering: uniform MUL/ADD/SHL -> SALU (s_mul_i32/s_add_i32/s_lshl_b32) in a scalar-temp SGPR pool (s64..s103); MOV_S2V at the vector boundary; gidx s{2+d} via s_mov (S_WGID). Lane-varying stays VALU.
finding: 14 syntactically-uniform int ops feed addresses (mostly stride muls). BUT on the LIVE address path the token index is OOB-clamped via where()->v_cndmask (a VECTOR op), so the address consumes the CLAMPED VGPR index, not the uniform pre-clamp value. Scalarizing the uniform prefix therefore produces values that the live address path does not consume.
